author: @simonsimpson4695
text: I would like to make a similar kind of thing for an elderly person. They find the joystick which is fitted on commercial chairs difficult because it's both throttle and direction. Steering and throttle separately seems a far better idea. This is for a home use scooter needing a small turning circle. If anyone has any suggestions on the kind of motor which would be needed, I'd be most grateful.  Scooters can be phenomenonally expensive and seeing this video makes me think it would be possible on a DIY basis. I have bicycle parts, welding machine etc and the skills to do it.

replies.author: @bigsteve6729
replies.text: If you were going to do it you'd have done it already

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: the motor I used on this was a 120W dc motor - you really need very little power to get around - the drive is a toothed belt - you would probably pay around £40 for the entire drive train, wheel and motor and could source parts for it from a scooter store online

replies.author: @simonsimpson4695
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering Thanks. I'll take the information. Any help with this project will be useful. Unfortunately I'm very busy with another huge project at the moment but as soon as it's finished I will turn my attention to this. It was great to see your video IRO the fact that not much power is needed.

replies.author: @simonsimpson4695
replies.text: @bigsteve6729 it's a good thing no one said this to Thomas Edison, Nikola Tesla, Tim Berners-Lee or Alan Turing. The world would be a different place. It's good motivation though. Thanks. Definitely doing it.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering

author: @ericdelevinquiere9902
text: I can only imagine my mother with one of those things, I definitely could not afford the insurance!

author: @-robo-
text: It can only be a crazy cart as people with disabilities need something rock solid for it to be a viable wheelchair.

replies.author: @AndreaDingbatt
replies.text: Nah,me and some of my friends would Definitly enjoy This!!  AX

replies.author: @-robo-
replies.text: @AndreaDingbatt As long as it is intended as such.  I have one friend in particular that pointed out the inherent dangers to someone who is wheelchair bound.  That said, I am not trying to rain on anyone's parade. Just say'n that it should carry an "entertainment only " disclaimer.

replies.author: @AndreaDingbatt
replies.text:
@-robo- Fair enough, I do understand what you mean.
And of course, even though I may enjoy it, putting my 87yr old mother in it,,I'm not certain that she would.... 

Thus this Would only be fun to me, and many of my silliness is best to be taken with a pinch of salt...

However, I'm a wheelchair user, and I would Love to have a go on this!!
As would some of my friends, as for other people, I can't speak for them, everyone is different, and in life there is no One Size Fits All...
I hope you have a wonderful weekend. AX

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I disagree mate to me people with disabilities are people too and sometimes people need fun - everything being 'rock solid' all the time is just plain dull and if I can say so - depressing - a simple lever to lock the rear wheels would make this a 'normal' chair - then it could be either depending on a persons mood

replies.author: @-robo-
replies.text: ​@ThinkingandTinkering As I mentioned in another comment, so long as there is a disclaimer that states "this is intended for entertainment purposes only". I am a person with disabilities (not pleased to reveal that in order to make my point) and I think I can speak for my friend and others when I say nothing is more depressing than ending up in a hospital bed.  While I hope that I am smart enough to know the difference, in this I am not sure I can speak for others. As I also mentioned as, I am not trying to rain on anyone's parade, this does indeed look like a ton of fun, however there should IMHO be a modicum of caution applied. Oh yeah, thank you for acknowledging that I am people too, although some of us disabled may have found that a tad bit patronizing.

author: @azlandpilotcar4450
text: Like sailing a small boat with no centerboard.  Fun.

author: @jasonhaymanonthedrawingboard
text: It’s one stage short of bumper cars! Love it! Flight simulator/ space trainers? Ad a few paintball guns and you can take all you mobility challenges to the next level? I think you just showed how cheaply and cost effective mobility can be. I wonder if Colin furze would take you guys up on a dodgems bout? Or go full wacky racers? In a e vehicle tournament. Around the world perhaps.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: To be honest mate - I think so too - mobility doesn't have to mean expense

replies.author: @jasonhaymanonthedrawingboard
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering even on a small scale it definitely expands peoples options. I’ve seen many verities or mobility options from tracks to skid steer. I think we should alway greet anyone in a wheel chair as m’lord or m’lady as the must be like kings or queen to have their own mobile throne. I dwelt and dreamt of a hover throne to do just that. seeing all the Dalek comment believe you might be on the right track. The m.o.d would be interested in a personal mini tank that spins on the spot. A 3inch plate version would be awesome to see. A multi plenum design should do the trick. With converging airflow. What is they say? Start small aim big! We rod you most certainly have done that. Does that mean Canterbury will the site of the world first hover tank factory? I know Star Wars and many an RPG have aspired to it! Crazy but that’s how it goes as Ozzie once sang. Fun how one simple thing raises up such possibilities. Q branch would be proud!
